phase1.explanation:
The File class represents file and directory paths:

Creating File objects:
• new File("filename") — relative path
• new File("/absolute/path") — absolute path
• new File("parent", "child") — path from parts

File methods:
• exists() — checks if file exists
• createNewFile() — creates empty file
• mkdir() — creates single directory
• mkdirs() — creates nested directories
• delete() — deletes file/directory
• renameTo(File) — renames file
• list() — lists files in directory
• listFiles() — returns File[]

File properties:
• getName() — filename
• getAbsolutePath() — full path
• getParent() — parent directory
• length() — file size in bytes
• lastModified() — last modified time
• isFile() / isDirectory() — type check
• canRead() / canWrite() — permissions

Path separators:
• File.separator — platform-independent separator (/, \, etc.)
• File.pathSeparator — list separator (;, :)

Warning: delete() only works on empty directories!
Warning: renameTo() might fail between different filesystems!

phase2.errorMessage:
Deleted: false

(No error — but the file WASN'T deleted! delete() returned false.)

phase3.prompt:
Write a program that:
1. Creates a directory called "data" (use mkdir())
2. Creates a file called "data/info.txt" (use createNewFile())
3. Checks if the file exists and prints its size
4. Lists all files in the "data" directory
5. Deletes the file and then the directory
